title: Apple pie
subtitle: Deep double-crust pie with Bramley apples and brown sugar
excerpt: A deep double-crust pie filled with Bramley apples tossed in brown sugar and mixed spice. The apples collapse and caramelise beneath the pastry lid. Serve warm with cream or custard. Makes one 23 cm pie.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: apple pie documented as a household standard with a double shortcrust case and a Bramley or cooking apple filling sweetened with sugar. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): apple pie as a weekly English household pudding is recorded across multiple regional entries. The Bramley apple is the British cooking apple for this purpose, noted in both sources as collapsing to a thick, sharp purée during baking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked: assemble the pie, wrap, and freeze. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan adding 15–20 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry can be made and refrigerated 48 hours ahead. The filling can be made a day ahead and kept covered.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 210°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: A small cut or opening made in the lid of a covered pie before baking. As the filling heats, steam builds up inside the pie. Without a vent, the pressure builds and can crack the pastry or cause the lid to lift away from the sides. A central hole or a few slashes let the steam escape, keeping the lid in place and the pastry crisp.

content.text: Bramley apples are the correct choice for a British apple pie. They collapse and turn to a soft, sharp purée under the pastry, which is what you want — not crunchy apple slices in a pastry case. If you cannot find Bramleys, use eating apples but add a squeeze of lemon and reduce the sugar slightly.

items.prepNote: peeled, cored, and thickly sliced, about 5–6 large

content.text: Make the pastry and prepare the filling

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add 3–4 tbsp cold water and press to a smooth dough. Divide into two portions, one slightly larger. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes. Toss the {{apple-bramley}} slices with the {{soft-brown-sugar}}, {{mixed-spice}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, and {{cornflour}}.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the larger pastry portion to line a deep 23 cm pie dish, allowing the pastry to overhang the edges. Fill with the apple mixture, mounding it slightly in the centre. Roll the second portion for the lid and place over the apples. Press the edges together and trim. Make a

in the centre. Brush with the beaten {{eggs}} and scatter with {{caster-sugar}} (finishing amount). Bake for 45–50 minutes, until the pastry is deep gold and the filling is bubbling through the vent. Rest for 15 minutes before serving.
